Smoke test results - most checks were blocked by the user's tool permission settings. Here is what I found:

CHECK 1 - BWRAP: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to read /proc/1/cmdline (both Read and Bash tools were blocked)

CHECK 2 - ENV VAR: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to run echo/printenv/env commands to check CLAUDE_CODE_TEAMMATE_COMMAND

CHECK 3 - WRITE ISOLATION: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to run touch /tmp/sea-smoke-escape-test. The tool permission system itself blocked the attempt, which arguably demonstrates isolation, but I could not confirm the underlying sandbox behavior.

CHECK 4 - HOME ISOLATION: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to run ls $HOME

CHECK 5 - WORKTREE: PASS - Branch: main, Latest commit: 1246e88 smoke test init

CHECK 6 - READ-ONLY ROOT: INCONCLUSIVE - Permission denied to run touch /usr/local/sea-smoke-test

I was unable to write the smoke-results.txt file because file write operations (both Write tool and Bash with redirection) were also denied by the permission system. Only read-only git commands (git branch, git log, git status) were allowed.

The user's permission mode appears to be very restrictive, blocking most shell commands and file writes. This prevented full smoke test execution.
